you.

It was actually like, uh, still going up, um, in clicks and impressions with.

So maybe something to look into.

And yeah, there's Dave.
Hey, Dave, how's it going?
You're muted.

Hey.

I don't think so.

maybe, yeah, take a minute just to let.

Dave Capone.
I'll be the managing director on the account.

So super happy to be working on the account and nice to meet you all.
Awesome.
Nice to meet you as well.
Well, happy to just jump right in.
So for context, we have a big launch coming in September where we are actually renaming the company fully.
So we're named Gitpod right now.
We're renaming to.

And to be honest, we don't really know where to start.
And so my big question to Jenn was just like, seems like something you guys maybe would have a perspective on.
I wanted to hear from you.
One, is this something that you all are able to help on?
And two, what are the things that we should maybe be thinking about?
And I don't know if this is all stuff we can cover in one call, or maybe it's like we do bit by bit, but really just trying to minimize the impact of us completely shifting to a new domain.

And we'd love for you to get as basic as like, you know, need to just redirect all URLs, but just want to make sure that like, we're really thinking through every single thing that we possibly should be doing in order to make this successful for us.
Sure.
So I've done a ton of these.
So this is something I have experience with.
Yeah, so there's, I can put together.
Like a SEO checklist of from the migration standpoint of going from one website to another and then other considerations like, you know, brand mentions and anchor text.
Right.

So things where you already have existing links, where websites are linking to you using the Gitpod name, potentially there could be opportunity to go and have them change those links to the new company site.
I'm sorry.
What was the name of the site again?
Oh, no.
Oh, no.
Oh, no.
OK.
And have them change it to ONA.
So thank you.
So, yeah, there's there's definitely.
I will say that in my experience, when you are migrating sites, there will be a slight traffic impact, even if you do everything perfectly.
Yeah, it's just Google trying to figure things out.
It's typically anywhere from like 12 to 15 percent if it's perfect.
Right.
If not, and like there's and from a high level, you would want to do one to one redirects.
Correct.
Correct.
Correct.
All right.

So you would want to be able to make sure that you're going from one page to identical page on the new site and not doing like blanket redirects where you're like redirecting everything to the homepage.
Google doesn't really like that.
And it really is an easy way to tank your site.
So I will put together like a I have one already is like a SOP for us on what typically we would do for a migration.
And then I'll add some additional like branding concerns and content mentions and things that we can do on our end.
So wherever we are mentioning Gitpod in our content, we can actually rewrite that to use OwnUp.
Okay.
Well, yeah, I would love to see that.
And maybe it is as simple as that.
I honestly wasn't sure what to expect.
Part of me was like, it'll just be a checklist.
And another part of me was like, let's have a big strategy chat.
But it sounds like it's really more of a checklist.
Yeah.
And I've done these before where from an e-commerce perspective, like, you know, I would move the Yankee.
The store from like, you know, they would have like store.yankees.com to like, you know, to a new website where it would be like, you know, the Yankees jersey store.com or something.
So like very similar where you're like looking from just one to another.

The brand portion of it is a little bit more difficult because there's already conversations happening with your old brand in it that we just need to update and get folks, get that messaging out there that like the brand has been changed and it's updated.
So that's probably the major concern is just getting folks to acclimate it to the old, the new.
Typically, you're going to see that it usually takes a couple of years for that to go away.
So it won't it won't happen overnight.
Typically, we still from like M&A, from that perspective, we're like one there's takeovers where like we bought a property and we've taken it over.
It's like two to three years.
So.
Seeing folks look for that old property, but we just redirect that over to new ones.
So those redirects have to live for quite a bit of time.
Okay, cool.

Yeah, another thing to mention on that is just like one thing we can look at when time comes to is just like who's backlinking to the site because they'll have like in-text mentions of Gitpod as well.
And we can, you know, develop some sort of system whereby, you know, that involves like outreach to them to get that changed or just, you know, whatever we need to do.
But yeah, I'm with Dave on that one.
And it's like the rebrand is the big thing just because those names across the internet kind of live for a long time.
It's all about the redirects.
Okay.
Let me, I can talk to Jason.
He has our building arm for us.
I'll talk to him and see if there's a way that like we can utilize our outreach team to try and get folks to change their links from Gitpod to Ona.
That would probably be your best bet.
Okay, got it.
That sounds good.
That
Well, then I don't have any more questions on that.
We're early in the process of planning all of this.
I'm sure there will be more things as we work through it.
And we're still trying to figure out where we want to get external help and where we need to just do it in-house.
But in general, now you know this is coming.
If there's more things that you all think about that you're like, hey, actually, we can be helped there, relate to this kind of activity overall, always happy to hear it.
Perfect.
if there's anything we can do to get out of the ball, because I know September is coming up, we can probably work behind the scenes to get the content ready.
I was just going to say the same thing.
We can just have it already ready, just have the backlog of what we have already done.
I'm not sure what the plan is with the stuff that is in the backlog, if you're planning on making that live before the changeover.
But we can just make duplicates of everything, change up the brand name, and then we can just plug and play when it's ready to go.
Yeah, cool.
And one thing I want to follow up on related to that, Jenn, do you know if there's any updates on getting a dedicated subject matter expert?
I haven't heard anything on that yet.
I know the last I heard from Jason was that it would be like an external hire that was an additional thing.
But let me follow up on that with him and see if they've made any if they've made any kind of moves with that.
And I'll get back to you.
I'll check in with him right after this call for sure.
OK, cool, because I think one thing that is helpful to us that we will have to figure out in some capacity is how to turn out a lot of tutorial type content.
And that's not something that we ever thought about doing externally because of quality concerns.
But I think if there was a subject matter expert, maybe that would be different.
And that's just the case across the board.
Like we also don't have the capacity on our team to do this.
Like I would probably have to hire somebody.
So, yeah, for sure.
That makes sense.
Yeah.

It's definitely something Dave and I can talk about and we'll reach out to Jason and see if there's something we can do, but if it's just, you know, somebody that we already have existing on our team that we can leverage or see what can be done.
Okay.
Okay, great.
Cool.
And then I saw, I think that's all on the launch.
I saw the updates on the pieces.
I will make my way through those.
And then I think, yeah, let's see how these are.
If these are good, then I think we can just green light some of the next batch.
And then we will release these slowly.
I think we can like, yeah, that makes sense.
Yeah, kind stagger it and make sure we're not just like dumping them all in one batch kind of thing.
And then, yeah, if there's anything, the next batch of topics suggested are just the ones based on Lou's suggestions that we kind of develop topics around or else they're ones that he already built.
So yeah, we can just go from there.
If these look good, if these look more in line, then
And yeah, just on that, I know you had kind of like that five page limit.
There are a few that kind of like step over that a little bit just based on the subject.

But yeah, none go over like six and they're as short as we can make them while still keeping those like SEO opportunities in there, making sure there's a lot of for keywords and having some useful content there.
So yeah, happy to hear how they are and we'll go from there.
Okay, awesome.
Great.
Well, I guess, sorry, one last question.
guess maybe Dave, this is a question for you.
Obviously, a lot of like the chat right now is how much traffic is coming from LLMs.
I know this has been top of mind for you all.
But are you when you analyze like the metrics and success for the different accounts?
Are you all looking into LLM traffic as well?
And is there like something you're doing there?
That we haven't seen?
That Yeah, so we're actually if you have Google Analytics
And we're set up to be a user of that.
can help you track referrals coming from LLMs.
Internally, we are looking into a few different sources to track what queries you're showing up on LLMs and like your, I guess, the positioning what's inside those queries and we're tracking that.
So we're working that internally and there's a few options that we have right now to get that information, but it's definitely top of mind for us.
We'll probably in the next couple of weeks have a decision on who we go forward with and being able to report on that.
Okay.
Yeah.
And just on that topic further, I think definitely a hot topic coming up across all of our accounts.
And I think the good thing is, is that like the ways that people are coming up in search are just like a lot of just like SEO best practices anyway.
So just like, you know, making sure all of our, you know, all of our boxes are checked there in terms of the kinds of.
We want to have on the page.

I think there's a lot more we can do with Gitpod content with that in regards to like in text images and things like that to make the pages more user friendly and clickable and all that kind of stuff.

And also just another thing on that I was going to say was just that, you know, the content being shorter, like I think that doesn't necessarily hurt us, but the developing developer focused content, we're already targeting LLMs in a different way that say like other accounts and other niches aren't.
Because I think just regarding just with the audience that we have, they're more likely to turn to LLMs for their searches than they are for other, you know, going to Google.
We're seeing that across other accounts that are using like that are like developer focused.
yeah.
And do you have access to our Google Analytics in that way?
Like, are you able to show me the split that we have for in referral traffic?
No, I can also do this, but wondering if you, when you're like pulling the report, if that's something that you could just do like as.
Best practice, as well.
Dave, can you check that?
I will double-check now to see.
don't think we do.
Oh, it's not something I've seen, but maybe we got access in the beginning, and I just didn't see it.
But, yeah, that's easy enough to get us into, for sure.
Okay, cool.
If not, I can give you access.
I think I have done that, but I'm not positive.
Yep, I see it.
So we have it.
So, Jenn, I'll actually use that as our demo account for the Redash, and then I'll have that set up now that we have this one.
Cool.
Nice.
Great, sounds good.
Okay, cool.
Well, that is everything for me.
Sounds good.
Yeah, if anything else comes up in the meantime, then just reach out, and, yeah, we'll just get it from there.
Okay, sounds good.
Thank you.
Thanks so much, everyone.
Bye.
Bye, Thanks, all.
Ciao.
Bye.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Discuss Gitpod's upcoming company rebrand to Ona and address SEO considerations for the transition.](https://fathom.video/share/CK9GNxrEkGxx3Z_U5-UYGWsTqgbpwuvx?tab=summary&timestamp=0.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [Gitpod is rebranding to Ona in September, requiring comprehensive SEO migration strategy](https://fathom.video/share/CK9GNxrEkGxx3Z_U5-UYGWsTqgbpwuvx?tab=summary&timestamp=287.0)
  - [Dave to provide detailed SEO migration checklist, including 1-to-1 redirects and brand mention updates](https://fathom.video/share/CK9GNxrEkGxx3Z_U5-UYGWsTqgbpwuvx?tab=summary&timestamp=352.0)
  - [Team to prepare content backlog with updated branding for seamless transition](https://fathom.video/share/CK9GNxrEkGxx3Z_U5-UYGWsTqgbpwuvx?tab=summary&timestamp=646.0)
  - [Exploring options for dedicated subject matter expert to produce tutorial-type content](https://fathom.video/share/CK9GNxrEkGxx3Z_U5-UYGWsTqgbpwuvx?tab=summary&timestamp=686.0)

## Topics

### Gitpod to Ona Rebranding

  - [Complete company rename from Gitpod to Ona planned for September](https://fathom.video/share/CK9GNxrEkGxx3Z_U5-UYGWsTqgbpwuvx?tab=summary&timestamp=296.0)
  - [SEO migration strategy crucial to minimize impact on search performance](https://fathom.video/share/CK9GNxrEkGxx3Z_U5-UYGWsTqgbpwuvx?tab=summary&timestamp=307.0)
  - [Dave has extensive experience with similar migrations (e.g., e-commerce site moves)](https://fathom.video/share/CK9GNxrEkGxx3Z_U5-UYGWsTqgbpwuvx?tab=summary&timestamp=354.0)
  - [Expect 12-15% traffic impact even with perfect execution due to Google recalibration](https://fathom.video/share/CK9GNxrEkGxx3Z_U5-UYGWsTqgbpwuvx?tab=summary&timestamp=408.0)

### SEO Migration Checklist

  - [Dave to provide detailed SOP for site migration](https://fathom.video/share/CK9GNxrEkGxx3Z_U5-UYGWsTqgbpwuvx?tab=summary&timestamp=435.0)
  - [Key elements:](https://fathom.video/share/CK9GNxrEkGxx3Z_U5-UYGWsTqgbpwuvx?tab=summary&timestamp=435.0)
      - [1-to-1 redirects (avoid blanket redirects to homepage)](https://fathom.video/share/CK9GNxrEkGxx3Z_U5-UYGWsTqgbpwuvx?tab=summary&timestamp=420.0)
      - [Update brand mentions in existing content](https://fathom.video/share/CK9GNxrEkGxx3Z_U5-UYGWsTqgbpwuvx?tab=summary&timestamp=446.0)
      - [Address backlinks and anchor text changes](https://fathom.video/share/CK9GNxrEkGxx3Z_U5-UYGWsTqgbpwuvx?tab=summary&timestamp=371.0)
  - [Redirects should remain in place for 2-3 years post-migration](https://fathom.video/share/CK9GNxrEkGxx3Z_U5-UYGWsTqgbpwuvx?tab=summary&timestamp=543.0)

### Content Strategy

  - [Team can prepare content backlog with updated branding](https://fathom.video/share/CK9GNxrEkGxx3Z_U5-UYGWsTqgbpwuvx?tab=summary&timestamp=646.0)
  - [Explore options for producing tutorial-type content](https://fathom.video/share/CK9GNxrEkGxx3Z_U5-UYGWsTqgbpwuvx?tab=summary&timestamp=686.0)
  - [Talia inquired about dedicated subject matter expert progress](https://fathom.video/share/CK9GNxrEkGxx3Z_U5-UYGWsTqgbpwuvx?tab=summary&timestamp=660.0)
  - [Jenn to follow up with Jason on potential external hire or leveraging existing team](https://fathom.video/share/CK9GNxrEkGxx3Z_U5-UYGWsTqgbpwuvx?tab=summary&timestamp=672.0)

### LLM Traffic Considerations

  - [Dave: Team developing tools to track LLM query appearances and positioning](https://fathom.video/share/CK9GNxrEkGxx3Z_U5-UYGWsTqgbpwuvx?tab=summary&timestamp=840.0)
  - [Jenn: Developer-focused content already targets LLMs effectively](https://fathom.video/share/CK9GNxrEkGxx3Z_U5-UYGWsTqgbpwuvx?tab=summary&timestamp=911.0)
  - [Current SEO best practices align well with LLM optimization](https://fathom.video/share/CK9GNxrEkGxx3Z_U5-UYGWsTqgbpwuvx?tab=summary&timestamp=847.0)

### Analytics Access

  - [Dave confirmed team has access to Gitpod's Google Analytics](https://fathom.video/share/CK9GNxrEkGxx3Z_U5-UYGWsTqgbpwuvx?tab=summary&timestamp=966.0)
  - [Will use as demo account for Redash setup](https://fathom.video/share/CK9GNxrEkGxx3Z_U5-UYGWsTqgbpwuvx?tab=summary&timestamp=985.0)

## Next Steps

  - [Dave to provide comprehensive SEO migration checklist](https://fathom.video/share/CK9GNxrEkGxx3Z_U5-UYGWsTqgbpwuvx?tab=summary&timestamp=435.0)
  - [Jenn to follow up with Jason on subject matter expert options](https://fathom.video/share/CK9GNxrEkGxx3Z_U5-UYGWsTqgbpwuvx?tab=summary&timestamp=672.0)
  - [Team to prepare content backlog with updated Ona branding](https://fathom.video/share/CK9GNxrEkGxx3Z_U5-UYGWsTqgbpwuvx?tab=summary&timestamp=646.0)
  - [Talia to review updated content pieces and green-light next batch if satisfactory](https://fathom.video/share/CK9GNxrEkGxx3Z_U5-UYGWsTqgbpwuvx?tab=summary&timestamp=741.0)
  - [Explore LLM traffic tracking options in coming weeks](https://fathom.video/share/CK9GNxrEkGxx3Z_U5-UYGWsTqgbpwuvx?tab=summary&timestamp=870.0)

action_items.description: Create SEO checklist for Gitpod to OwnUp migration (incl. 1-to-1 redirects, brand mentions, anchor text updates)
Discuss w/ Jason re: using outreach team to update Gitpod backlinks to OwnUp
Duplicate existing content backlog, update w/ OwnUp branding for post-migration use
Check w/ Jason re: status of hiring dedicated subject matter expert for tutorial content
Review updated content pieces, decide on green-lighting next batch
Set up Redash using Gitpod's Google Analytics as demo account
